name: Grilled Lemon-Pepper Chicken Salad
recipeInstructions:
1. Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, Tabasco sauce, and dried thyme in a small saucepan over low heat. Season the dressing to taste with salt and pepper.
2. Arrange chicken and onion slices on a plate. Spoon 2 tablespoons of dressing over the chicken and onions, turning to coat.
3. Let the chicken stand for 5 minutes. (The dish can be prepared up to 4 hours in advance. Cover and refrigerate.)
4. Prepare the barbecue grill at medium-high heat.
5. Place the saucepan with the remaining dressing at the edge of the grill to warm.
6. Grill the chicken and onion slices until just cooked through, about 5-6 minutes per side.
7. Transfer the grilled chicken and onions to a plate. Cut the chicken crosswise into thin slices and separate the onion into rings.
8. Place the lettuce in a large bowl. Add the chicken and onion.
9. Add the warm dressing and toss to coat. Season the salad to taste with salt and pepper.
10. Divide the salad between 2 plates. Sprinkle half of the feta cheese over each plate and serve.
